profiles.trustBoundary: Static graph artifacts may provide advisory Workspace graph evidence; live Graphify, MCP, watch mode, hooks, cache, and regeneration are not verifier inputs.
profiles.repairHint: Keep declared capability fields, graph evidence docs, fixtures, and Workspace tests aligned before claiming support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Codex manual executor readiness is human-mediated/manual and not a guarantee that a runnable local Codex CLI exists; local config, slash commands, hooks, plugins, goals, subagents, and app-server availability are not verifier inputs.
profiles.repairHint: Keep Executor Contract docs, Capability Request fixtures, and Workspace tests aligned before claiming support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Codex config may describe operator affordances such as sandboxing, approvals, apps, MCP servers, skills, hooks, and multi-agent tools; config is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh official Codex config references, Workspace boundary docs, and registry tests before changing this profile.

profiles.trustBoundary: Codex app-server APIs can describe threads, goals, tools, skills, plugins, MCP servers, and config state for an operator; app-server state is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh official Codex app-server references and keep any Workspace use framed as manual evidence recording only.

profiles.trustBoundary: Agentic Search tool taxonomy can guide a Codex operator choosing file search, skill loading, database query/search, web search, memory, or shell context sources, but retrieved context, local tool availability, Codex config, app-server state, and tool output are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

toolClasses.corePurpose: Find repo files, docs, source code, and artifacts through exact or structured local search.
toolClasses.triggerCondition: Use when the user request depends on source-specific local files, BMAD artifacts, or repository text.
toolClasses.negativeTriggerCondition: Do not use when the authoritative source is live web, persistent memory, or a database-only record.
toolClasses.authorityBoundary: Retrieved file context is advisory only; not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

toolClasses.corePurpose: Load task-specific instructions, examples, and constraints only when the task calls for that skill.
toolClasses.triggerCondition: Use when a named skill or complex tool workflow requires detailed procedure beyond the initial prompt.
toolClasses.negativeTriggerCondition: Do not load unrelated skills or keep obsolete skill bodies active after the task path changes.
toolClasses.authorityBoundary: Loaded skill context is advisory procedure; not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

toolClasses.corePurpose: Retrieve, filter, or aggregate structured records from a declared database context source.
toolClasses.triggerCondition: Use when source-specific records, metadata filters, counts, groups, or validation searches belong in the database.
toolClasses.negativeTriggerCondition: Do not use for deterministic BMAD tests unless a self-contained fixture or declared capability request permits it.
toolClasses.authorityBoundary: Database results and query errors are advisory operator context; not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

toolClasses.corePurpose: Retrieve current or external online information when local artifacts are not the authoritative source.
toolClasses.triggerCondition: Use when freshness, public documentation, external facts, or online source attribution is required.
toolClasses.negativeTriggerCondition: Do not use web search for internal BMAD artifacts, local repo facts, or database records that should come from their own source.
toolClasses.authorityBoundary: Web results and citations are advisory evidence context; not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

toolClasses.corePurpose: Retrieve persistent user or project continuity facts when prior interaction context may matter.
toolClasses.triggerCondition: Use when continuity, preferences, or prior decisions are relevant and not fully present in current artifacts.
toolClasses.negativeTriggerCondition: Do not use memory as authority over repo files, BMAD artifacts, deterministic tests, or current user instructions.
toolClasses.authorityBoundary: Memory is continuity context only; not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

toolClasses.corePurpose: Inspect local files, run deterministic commands, call CLIs, and gather repeatable command evidence.
toolClasses.triggerCondition: Use when the task needs repo inspection, tests, validators, file search, or CLI-accessible context.
toolClasses.negativeTriggerCondition: Do not run unsafe mutation, hidden execution, secret exposure, live services, or broad environment changes without explicit scope.
toolClasses.authorityBoundary: Shell command output is operator evidence only; not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

profiles.repairHint: Keep Agentic Search glossary, planning doc, Forge metadata, registry profile, and tests aligned before claiming tool-stack readiness.

profiles.trustBoundary: Playwright CLI can provide browser automation evidence for a manual Codex operator, but wrapper output, screenshots, traces, dependency resolution, and live browser state are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: Playwright wrapper and npx were present during planning.
Wrapper help failed because @playwright/cli resolved an unavailable alpha dependency.

profiles.repairHint: Refresh the Playwright CLI wrapper dependency or pin a resolvable package before changing the unavailable alpha dependency observation.

profiles.trustBoundary: Agent Browser CLI can provide browser snapshots, screenshots, and interaction transcripts for a manual Codex operator, but command presence, daemon state, browser output, and local PATH checks are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: agent-browser command was not detected on PATH during planning.

profiles.repairHint: Install or expose the Agent Browser CLI, rerun PATH help evidence, and keep not detected on PATH recorded until command evidence exists.

profiles.trustBoundary: Browser Use IAB can expose in-app browser navigation, snapshots, screenshots, and interaction context for a manual Codex operator, but plugin availability, node_repl state, page state, and browser-client output are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: Browser Use plugin context and skill instructions were available during planning.

profiles.repairHint: Record explicit Browser Use IAB manual evidence before citing browser output; plugin context alone is advisory only.

profiles.trustBoundary: Computer Use MCP can expose desktop app inventory and UI automation context for a manual Codex operator, but app state, accessibility output, user interface actions, and MCP availability are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: Computer Use MCP list_apps responded during planning.

profiles.repairHint: Treat list_apps as app-inventory evidence only; record separate manual evidence before claiming broader desktop action support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Git CLI status and diff output can provide Workspace Worktree Review evidence for an operator; Git command output is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

commandEvidence.boundary: Informational operator evidence only; not verifier input, not support promotion, and not grant authority.

nonMutatingCommands.purpose: Capture Git CLI version used for manual evidence.
Capture canonical worktree root before status or diff inspection.
Capture dirty state, including staged, unstaged, and untracked paths.
Capture unstaged patch evidence without mutating repository state.
Capture staged patch evidence without mutating repository state.

profiles.repairHint: Keep Git Worktree Review contract fields, official Git references, review artifacts, fixture, and Workspace tests aligned before claiming support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Context7 MCP can provide docs evidence for an operator, including Git MCP package docs, but live MCP availability, Apple Passwords state, API key presence, docs output, and local Codex config are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh Context7 and Codex MCP docs, keep Apple Passwords item Context7 secret handling redacted, and record manual operator evidence before citing live Context7 output.

profiles.trustBoundary: Context7 MCP can provide scoped Google Apps Script docs evidence for an operator, including guide, reference, and samples sources, but live MCP availability, API key presence, local Codex config, Apps Script runtime state, trigger install, deployment state, and target repo manifests are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh the Apps Script Context7 source register, keep source labels mapped to exact Context7 library IDs, and record manual operator evidence before citing live Apps Script docs output.

profiles.trustBoundary: Context7 MCP can provide scoped WebGL Fundamentals docs evidence for an operator, including /websites/webglfundamentals and upstream WebGL2 caveat context, but live MCP availability, API key presence, local Codex config, WebGL runtime state, browser GPU state, docs output, and any separate WebGL2 source claim are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh the WebGL Fundamentals Context7 source register, keep /websites/webglfundamentals as the canonical source, preserve the WebGL2 caveat, and record manual operator evidence before citing live WebGL docs output.

profiles.trustBoundary: Docker MCP Toolkit can help an operator author Docker MCP Gateway profiles and Context7 server setup, but live Docker MCP Gateway state, Docker MCP Catalog state, Docker secrets, secretRef resolution, Apple Passwords state, Context7 API key presence, network access, MCP access, and local Docker CLI shape are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh official Docker MCP and Context7 references before changing support claims; keep Docker MCP Toolkit version-dependent and secret placeholders limited to <CONTEXT7_API_KEY> or <redacted>.

profiles.trustBoundary: Desktop Commander MCP can expose zsh-capable terminal evidence for an operator, but live MCP availability, GitHub star count drift, defaultShell state, allowedDirectories state, shell command output, host filesystem access, process control, Codex config, npm state, Docker state, and remote MCP state are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh Desktop Commander star threshold, README, SECURITY, and Codex MCP references before changing support claims; keep zsh shell evidence non-mutating, grant-gated, secret-free, and advisory only.

profiles.trustBoundary: PostgreSQL MCP can expose operator-provided read-only database query evidence through an archived/deprecated reference server, but live PostgreSQL state, Docker MCP runtime state, connection strings, query results, local MCP config, network access, and Codex config are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh PostgreSQL MCP source refs, keep read-only and secret boundaries explicit, and record manual evidence before citing database observations.

profiles.trustBoundary: Git MCP can expose local repository status, diff, add, commit, and branch tools, but live MCP availability, repository dirty state, write tools, and GitHub connector state are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Configure mcp-server-git only as an operator-granted local Git tool, record manual evidence, and keep add, commit, and branch actions grant-gated before claiming support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Google Calendar remote MCP can expose operator-granted Calendar event tools, but live MCP server discovery, OAuth state, Codex connector availability, target repo manifests, Calendar API enablement, trigger install, deployment state, and Codex config are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh official Google Calendar MCP docs, source register, fixture, capability contract, and tests before changing support claims.

profiles.trustBoundary: Outlook Calendar remote MCP can expose operator-granted Microsoft Graph calendar tools, but live MCP server discovery, Outlook Calendar connector availability, Microsoft Graph permission state, Office.js add-in runtime state, local OAuth setup, token cache, mailbox state, event payloads, and Codex config are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

profiles.trustBoundary: Graphify query, path, and explain commands may help an operator navigate committed graph artifacts; command output is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

commandEvidence.boundary: Informational operator evidence only; not verifier input, not support promotion, and not grant authority.
commandEvidence.graphFormat: Graphify CLI smoke tests use Graphify-native node-link JSON with nodes[] and links[]; BMAD normalized graph artifacts use nodes[] and edges[] and are not CLI smoke fixtures.

smokeTests.command: uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify query "capability evidence" --graph test/fixtures/graphify/native-node-link.graph.json
uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify explain "Capability Evidence" --graph test/fixtures/graphify/native-node-link.graph.json
uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify path "Capability Evidence" "Workspace Verifier" --graph test/fixtures/graphify/native-node-link.graph.json

profiles.repairHint: Keep Graphify command references, graph evidence docs, and Workspace tests aligned before claiming query-profile support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Graphify MCP can expose structured graph access for an operator, but live MCP activation is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Keep MCP references advisory, and require explicit manual evidence before any Workspace result mentions live graph-tool output.

profiles.trustBoundary: Graphify hooks, watch mode, cache refresh, and regeneration may explain graph freshness risk; automation output is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

commandEvidence.boundary: Informational operator evidence only; not verifier input, not support promotion, and not grant authority.

smokeTests.command: uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify hook status

profiles.repairHint: Promote only after committed Workspace evidence proves explicit, bounded hook or regeneration handling without hidden execution.
